data.title: U.S. Treasury Yields Decline Ahead of Employment Report
data.description: According to PANews, U.S. Treasury yields experienced a decline due to technical issues at the Bureau of Labor Statistics, occurring just before the release of the non-farm employment report. Concurrently, the emerging markets currency index reached its highest point of the day.

data.title: Bitcoin Futures Funding Rates Show Signs of Cooling
data.description: According to Foresight News, data from Glassnode indicates that Bitcoin futures funding rates are experiencing a cooling trend. Currently, the rates stand at approximately $366,000 per hour, nearing the neutral threshold of $300,000 per hour for this cycle. A drop below this level would suggest weakening demand and highlight a growing divergence from the previous bullish phase's off-chain data.

data.title: Hyperliquid will introduce a voting mechanism for the stablecoin USDH and reduce some spot fees in its next network upgrade.
data.description: According to official news, Hyperliquid will optimize the spot market structure in the next network upgrade. Taker fees, maker rebates, and user trading volume contributions for spot trades between two spot-quoted assets will be uniformly reduced by 80% to improve liquidity and reduce user friction. Furthermore, the protocol's currently reserved stablecoin symbol, USDH, will be released through an on-chain validator voting process. This voting process will be conducted entirely on Hyperliquid L1 and will be similar to the delisting vote. Selected teams will then participate in the regular spot deployment gas auction. Officials stated that USDH, as a highly demanded regulated symbol, will be used to build a compliant, Hyperliquid-first native stablecoin. In the future, spot-quoted assets will gradually become permissionless, with related staking and penalty mechanisms to be announced later.

data.title: Yunfeng Financial Appoints New Independent Non-Executive Director
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Yunfeng Financial (00376.HK) has announced the appointment of Liang Xinjun as an independent non-executive director, effective from September 5, 2025.

Liang Xinjun currently serves as the Chairman and CEO of Xin Family Office. He is also known as a co-founder of Fosun Group and has extensive investment experience in areas such as Web3 blockchain, artificial intelligence, and the metaverse.

data.title: U.S. Senate Prepares Crypto Market Reform Bill for Review
data.description: According to PANews, the U.S. Senate Banking Committee is finalizing a draft for crypto market structure reform, with plans to present it to the committee this month and potentially hold a full Senate vote by the end of the year. The draft incorporates feedback from industry stakeholders and lobbying groups, marking the first significant involvement of the Democratic Party in this process. Meanwhile, an internal investigation at the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) revealed that text messages from former Chairman Gary Gensler, spanning from October 2022 to September 2023, were permanently deleted due to an IT error. These messages contained crucial information related to crypto enforcement, banking, and coordination with the White House. In response, the SEC has disabled text messaging for employees and enhanced training on record-keeping.

data.title: World Gold Council Proposes Digital Gold Amid Market Concerns
data.description:
According to PANews, Thu Lan Nguyen, Head of Foreign Exchange and Commodity Research at Commerzbank, highlighted a new initiative by the World Gold Council aimed at modernizing the gold market through the introduction of digital gold. This move is possibly driven by concerns that stablecoins and central bank digital currencies (CBDCs) might emerge as alternative investment options to gold. However, Nguyen argues that these concerns are unfounded.

Stablecoins and CBDCs are both tied to fiat currencies—stablecoins are backed by the U.S. dollar, while CBDCs are directly issued by central banks, functioning similarly to cash. In contrast, gold is considered 'politically neutral' as it is not issued by any central bank or political institution. The total supply of gold is primarily determined by mining output, which changes at a relatively slow pace.

For investors who choose gold to hedge against political risks, the ease and cost-effectiveness of transferring fiat currency may not be a primary concern. Gold's appeal lies in its independence from political influence, making it a unique asset in the investment landscape.

data.title: Planet Evening News | September 5
data.description: 1. Trader Eugene: SOL long position closed, market remains volatile; 2. The Ethereum Foundation completed a partial sell-off, withdrawing 3.387 million DAI from CEX; 3. Gemini launched ETH and SOL staking and perpetual contract services in the EU; 4. The National Natural Science Foundation of China released the 2025 Stablecoin Risk Governance Research Project Application Guide; 5. Justin Sun transferred $9 million worth of unlocked WLFI to HTX; 6. BitMine: Not subject to Nasdaq's requirements for crypto treasury companies to raise funds through share placements; 7. Binance Wallet will launch the Starpower (STAR) TGE on September 6; 8. Sora Ventures announced the launch of a Bitcoin reserve fund, planning to purchase $1 billion in Bitcoin within 6 months; 9. Binance HODLer airdrop launched on OpenLedger (OPEN); 10. Putian Hanjiang Prosecutor's Office sentenced a virtual currency underground foreign exchange case involving over $133 million. 11. HSBC: Hong Kong may have a RMB stablecoin in the future, and some large state-owned enterprises are actively exploring the application of stablecoins; 12. Bitwise analyst: Pokémon cards may become the next on-chain RWA market.

data.title: Lombard's governance token BARD completes community sale, with total subscription amount reaching $94.7 million
data.description: According to the Buidlpad platform, the BARD community sale for Lombard's governance token has concluded. The sale raised a total of $94.7 million, exceeding the original fundraising target of $6.75 million by 14 times. The sale attracted 133,357 participants from 132 countries, with 21,341 participants ultimately meeting the requirements. The oversubscribed $87.95 million has been returned to participants' Buidlpad wallets.

data.title: Anthony Scaramucci Admits He Failed To Understand Bitcoin For 8 Years Before Finally Becoming A Believer: 'I Was Very Negative'
data.description:
Anthony Scaramucci, the founder and CEO of SkyBridge Capital, shared on Thursday his journey from being a cryptocurrency skeptic to an advocate, attributing the shift to diligent research and understanding. 
source: https://www.benzinga.com/crypto/cryptocurrency/25/09/47518844/anthony-scaramucci-admits-he-failed-to-understand-bitcoin-for-8-years-before-finally-becoming-a-believer-i-was-very-negative?utm_source=cryptopanic.com&utm_campaign=partner_feed&utm_medium=partner_feed&utm_content=site

data.title: Monero '51% Attackers' Qubic Release AI Model—But It Can't Do Basic Math Yet
data.description:
Qubic attempted a 51% attack on Monero while training its AI model AIGarth. It's now posting on social media—and the public isn’t impressed.
source: https://decrypt.co/338223/monero-51-attackers-qubic-release-ai-model-but-it-cant-do-basic-math-yet

data.description:
With some real firepower, big backing and huge partners already lined up, this will be a new L1 blockchain to take very seriously.
source: https://decrypt.co/338222/morning-minute-stripes-l1-blockchain-tempo-goes-live-sort-of

data.title: Asia’s $1B Bitcoin Fund Launched by Sora Ventures in Bold Institutional Play
data.description:
Sora Ventures has launched Asia’s $1B Bitcoin Fund with a $200M institutional commitment, aiming to build a centralized treasury pool within six months
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/asias-1b-bitcoin-fund-launched-by-sora-ventures-in-bold-institutional-play/

data.title: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics Faces Technical Issues
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics is currently experiencing technical difficulties, resulting in the unavailability of its data retrieval tools on the website.

data.title: Bullish Europe subsidiary obtains MiCA license in Germany
data.description: The European subsidiary of cryptocurrency trading platform Bullish has been granted a license under Germany’s Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) regulation. Bullish Europe received the license from Germany’s Federal Financial Supervisory Authority (BaFin), adding to its existing crypto brokerage and custodian licenses from the regulator. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Hyperliquid Foundation Announces Major Spot Trading Optimizations
data.description:
Hyperliquid Foundation to slash spot trading fees by 80% with USDH launch.
source: https://coincu.com/360191-hyperliquid-spot-trading-optimization

data.title: Rising Global Liquidity Suggests More Upside for Bitcoin
data.description:
Bitcoin’s tight link to global liquidity suggests the recent pullback may be short-lived, with M2 expansion pointing to fresh highs ahead.
source: https://www.blockhead.co/2025/09/05/rising-global-liquidity-suggests-more-upside-for-bitcoin/

data.title: DeFi Development expands SOL holdings to over 2 million with new acquisition
data.description:
DeFi Development Corp purchased 196,141 SOL at an average price of $202. 76 per token, bringing its total holdings to over 2 million SOL tokens.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/defi-development-expands-sol-holding/

data.title: PicWe received the Movement Foundation Ecosystem Grant, which will accelerate its development of RWA and full-chain liquidity infrastructure.
data.description: PicWe has received a non-dilutive grant of 868,160.000166 MOVE from the Movement Foundation. The grant will be used to accelerate PicWe's R&D efforts in RWA and full-chain liquidity infrastructure, enabling the implementation and adoption of the Movement ecosystem in scenarios such as RWA (real-world assets), stablecoins, and cross-chain payments.

data.title: Elliptic Launches Tool for Tracking Stablecoin Movement Across Multiple Blockchains
data.description:
According to PANews, blockchain analytics firm Elliptic has introduced a new tool designed to track the movement of stablecoins like USDT and USDC across various blockchains. This tool aims to assist major financial institutions and stablecoin issuers in conducting wallet audits and asset tracking. Several large banks have already adopted the tool, which is applicable to all stablecoin issuers.

Elliptic reports that USDT has the highest circulation on the Tron and Ethereum blockchains. The firm notes that criminals often use stablecoins to mitigate currency risks. Most stablecoin issuers have the capability to freeze or blacklist addresses. Last year, Tron, Tether, and TRM Labs collectively froze over $250 million in assets linked to criminal activities.

data.title: Stripe’s Tempo Begins Private Testing with Financial Giants Visa, Deutsche Bank, Standard Chartered
data.description:
Stripe officially launched Tempo, a payments-focused Layer 1 blockchain targeting 100,000+ transactions per second with major financial institutions including Visa, Deutsche Bank, and Standard Chartered serving as initial design partners following the $92 billion payments giant's strategic acquisitions of Bridge and Privy.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/stripes-tempo-begins-private-testing-with-financial-giants-visa-deutsche-bank-standard-chartered/
